info.name: DataMammoth API v2

item.name: Health
item.description: Service health and status endpoints. No authentication required.

request.description: Returns the service health status including database and Redis connectivity. No authentication required.

item.name: Servers
item.description: Manage VPS/cloud servers: provision, power actions, snapshots, metrics, firewall, and console access.

request.description: Returns a paginated list of servers owned by the authenticated user. Supports filtering by status, provider, and region, plus full-text search on hostname, label, and IP.
Provisions a new server asynchronously. Returns 202 Accepted with a task ID to track provisioning progress. Requires product_id and image_id; provide either zone_id or region.
Returns detailed server information including live status from the provider, usage metrics, and HATEOAS links to related resources.
Updates a server's hostname and/or label. At least one field must be provided.
Terminates a server asynchronously. Returns 202 Accepted with a task ID. The server will be marked as 'terminated' once the provider confirms deletion.
Sends a power-on command to the server. The server must not be terminated.
Sends a hard power-off command to the server. For a graceful shutdown, use the Shutdown action instead.
Reboots the server. This is equivalent to a power cycle.
Sends a graceful shutdown (ACPI) command to the server's operating system.
Lists all snapshots for a server, fetched from the underlying provider.
Creates a new snapshot of the server's current state. Requires a name (max 255 chars) and optional description.
Permanently deletes a snapshot from the server and underlying provider.
Restores (rolls back) a server to the state captured in the specified snapshot. The server must not be terminated.

Returns server metrics (CPU, RAM, disk, network) for a given time period. Source can be 'db' (stored metrics), 'agent' (agent-reported), or 'provider' (native monitoring). Valid periods: lastday, lastweek, lastmonth, lastyear.
Returns the server's audit log / event history from the provider. Supports filtering by date range and action type.
Returns a time-limited VNC/SSH console access URL for the server. The URL type depends on the provider. Not available for terminated servers.
Returns the current firewall configuration for the server. If no firewall is configured, returns an empty ruleset with status 'disabled'.
Replaces the entire firewall configuration for the server. Accepts an array of rules (max 100) and an optional status ('active' or 'disabled').
Lists available hosting zones (data center locations). Supports filtering by continent, country, and search. Root users see provider details; tenant users see only enabled zones.
Lists available OS images for a specific zone. Images are filtered by the zone's provider and tenant-level access controls. Supports filtering by family (ubuntu, debian, etc.) and search.

item.name: Tasks
item.description: Track asynchronous operations like server provisioning and termination.

request.description: Lists async tasks for the current tenant. Filter by status (pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled) or type (server.create, server.terminate, etc.).
Returns the current status and result of a specific async task. Poll this endpoint to track provisioning progress.
Cancels a pending or running task. Tasks that are already completed or failed cannot be cancelled.

item.name: Products
item.description: Product catalog: list, detail, options, addons, pricing, and categories. Most endpoints are public (no auth required).

request.description: Lists active products for a tenant's storefront. Public endpoint — no auth required, but x-tenant-id header or filter[tenant_id] is mandatory. Supports search and filtering by type, status, and category.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "VPS Pro 4",
  "slug": "vps-pro-4",
  "description": "High-performance VPS with 4 vCPU cores, 8GB RAM, and 200GB NVMe SSD.",
  "short_description": "4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 200GB NVMe",
  "type": "vps",
  "category": "VPS Hosting",
  "status": "active",
  "pricing": {
    "monthly": 19.99,
    "quarterly": 54.99,
    "annual": 199.99,
    "setup_fee": 0,
    "currency": "USD"
  },
  "specs": {
    "cpu": 4,
    "ram_gb": 8,
    "disk_gb": 200,
    "disk_type": "NVMe SSD",
    "bandwidth": "32TB"
  },
  "is_featured": true,
  "badge": "Popular",
  "features": ["DDoS Protection", "Full Root Access", "99.9% Uptime SLA"]
}

request.description: Creates a new product in the catalog. Admin-only endpoint requiring products.create permission. Supports configurable options and addons.
Returns detailed product information including pricing, specs, features, and HATEOAS links to options, addons, and pricing endpoints. Public endpoint.
Updates product fields. Admin-only endpoint requiring products.update permission. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields are left unchanged.
Deletes a product from the catalog. Admin-only endpoint requiring products.delete permission.
Lists configurable options (e.g., OS, control panel, extra storage) for a product. Each option has choices with optional price adjustments. Public endpoint.
Lists available addons (e.g., backups, monitoring, extra IPs) for a product. Each addon has its own billing cycle and price. Public endpoint.
Returns product pricing with tenant-specific markup applied. Shows both base pricing and retail pricing. Pass tenant_id query param or x-tenant-id header. Public endpoint.
Lists distinct product categories with product counts. Requires tenant_id via query param or x-tenant-id header. Public endpoint.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Invoices, subscriptions, orders, balance, payment methods, and promo code validation.

request.description: Returns a paginated list of the authenticated user's invoices. Supports filtering by status (paid, unpaid, overdue, cancelled, refunded).

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('invoice_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Returns detailed invoice information including line items and payment links.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Initiates payment for an invoice. Accepts gateway (stripe, paypal, balance, crypto) and optional payment_method_id.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('subscription_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Cancel or reactivate a subscription. Action must be 'cancel' or 'reactivate'. Cancelled subscriptions can be reactivated; terminated ones cannot.

request.description: Returns detailed order information including line items, pricing breakdown, and HATEOAS links.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Validates a promotional code and returns its discount type, value, scope, expiry, and applicability. Always returns 200 with a 'valid' boolean.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

item.name: Support
item.description: Ticket management, replies, CSAT feedback, departments, and knowledge base.

request.description: Returns a paginated list of the authenticated user's support tickets with replies.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('ticket_id', b.data[0].id); } }

body.raw:
{
  "subject": "Unable to SSH into my server",
  "department": "Technical Support",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "message": "I am unable to SSH into my server at 192.168.1.1. Connection times out after 30 seconds. I have verified my SSH key is correct.",
  "category": "connectivity"
}

request.description: Creates a new support ticket. Priority options: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT.

item.name: Account
item.description: User profile, password, API keys, sessions, 2FA, notifications, and activity log.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile including name, email, 2FA status, and HATEOAS links.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Updates the authenticated user's profile fields. Only provided fields are changed.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Changes the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password for verification. New password must be at least 8 characters.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Lists the authenticated user's API keys. Key hashes are never returned; only the prefix is shown.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('api_key_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Creates a new API key. The raw key is returned ONLY in this response and cannot be retrieved again. Save it immediately.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

if (pm.response.code === 201) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && b.data.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('api_key_id', b.data.id); } if (b.data && b.data.key) { pm.collectionVariables.set('api_key', b.data.key); } }

request.description: Revokes (soft-deletes) an API key by ID. The key will no longer authenticate requests.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Lists the authenticated user's active sessions with IP address, user agent, and expiry time.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('session_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Revokes a specific session, logging out that device/browser.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

request.description: Manage two-factor authentication. Actions: 'status' (check current state), 'enable' (turn on 2FA), 'disable' (turn off 2FA). Full TOTP enrollment should be done via the web UI.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

item.name: Admin
item.description: Administrative endpoints for user management, roles, tenants, invoices, servers, tickets, leads, audit log, dashboard stats, masquerade, and v1 usage tracking.

request.description: Lists users in the admin's tenant with pagination and search. Requires users.read permission.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('user_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Creates a new user in the admin's tenant. Requires users.create permission. Optionally assign roles at creation.

request.description: Lists all invoices across the tenant with user details. Supports search by invoice number or user email/name. Requires billing.read permission.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Webhook subscription management: create, update, delete, test, view deliveries, and list available event types.

request.description: Lists all webhook subscriptions for the current tenant. Secrets are masked (only last 6 chars shown).

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && Array.isArray(b.data) && b.data.length > 0) { pm.collectionVariables.set('webhook_id', b.data[0].id); } }

request.description: Creates a new webhook subscription. A signing secret is auto-generated and returned in the response. Store the secret securely for signature verification.

exec: pm.test('Response has v2 envelope', function() { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.have.property('meta'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('request_id'); pm.expect(b.meta).to.have.property('version', '2.0'); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var b = pm.response.json(); if (b.data && b.data.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('webhook_id', b.data.id); } }
